name: Animation-Orchestration Intent
summary: Intra-element build-in animations and how multiple of them relate in time — SVG draw-on, staggered list reveals, a chart growing in — distinct from slide-to-slide transition (#1183) and fragment step-reveal (#1181). Those are about navigation between or within slides; this is about how the content of a single moment animates into place: the sequencing (parallel / stagger / chain), the trigger (on-view / on-step / on-scroll), and composing the Motion intent so all of it honors prefers-reduced-motion. General, not deck-specific — any content surface (a marketing page, a dashboard) wants orchestrated build-ins. Homed in webintents per the ratified distributed placement (#1175).

sequence.description: How multiple element animations relate in time. parallel = all start together; stagger = each starts a fixed offset after the previous (a cascading list); chain = each starts when the previous finishes (a strict timeline).

trigger.description: What starts the orchestration. on-view = when the element scrolls/transitions into view (Intersection Observer); on-step = driven by an advance/fragment step (a deck build); on-scroll = scroll-linked progress, the continuous 0..1 driver owned by the Scroll Progress intent (/intents/scroll-progress/ — reference/axis/range, lowering to CSS Scroll-driven Animations). This trigger names the start condition; scroll-progress supplies the driven value.

replay.description: Whether the build-in repeats. once = play a single time and stay (the default); re-enter = replay each time the element re-enters view / the step is revisited.

description:
How content arrives

Beyond moving between slides, content builds in: an SVG path draws itself, a list staggers in, a chart grows. The Animation-Orchestration intent names how those intra-element build-ins are sequenced and triggered — a layer distinct from navigation.

Not transition, not fragment

  • Slide transition (#1183) animates the change between slides.
  • Fragment reveal (#1181) reveals slide content step-by-step under navigation.
  • This intent orchestrates how a single revealed element animates into place — the build-in itself, and how several relate in time.

Composition

Composes the Motion intent: every orchestrated build-in honors prefers-reduced-motion (instant placement when reduced). General across content surfaces — the deck is one consumer, not the owner.
